title: phragmites australis subsp. maritimus
intro: Phragmites australis subsp. maritimus, also known as coastal or sea club-rush, is a subspecies of the common reed plant that is typically found in coastal areas and is characterized by its tall, slender stems and feathery plumes. The medicinal properties of Phragmites australis subsp. maritimus include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat a range of health issues including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions such as asthma and bronchitis. Phragmites australis subsp. maritimus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
